query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed comprehensive research on the 2026 Johor state election. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**Election basics:** Polls July 11, 2026 (10 days out), a snap election called by incumbent BN MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi who dissolved the assembly June 1. All 56 seats contested; 29 needed to govern; ~2.73M voters (54% Malay, 36% Chinese, 7% Indian).

**Expected outcome — BN strongly favored:** Analyst consensus (FULCRUM/ISEAS, SIIA's Dr Oh Ei Sun, others) is that BN retains the Johor government. Teng Fong Securities scenarios put ~80% combined probability on BN retaining control and only ~5% on an outright opposition/PN win. BN holds a 40-seat supermajority from 2022.

**Green wave / PN-PAS dynamic — the key twist:** PN is NOT mounting a strong independent challenge. It contests only 33 seats and withdrew from ~23, and PAS *directed its supporters to vote BN* in seats where PN isn't running — consolidating the Malay-Muslim vote behind BN ("a gift to UMNO") rather than producing an independent green-wave surge. Bersatu has collapsed (won just 2 of 33 in 2022). The main risk is the inverse: analysts warn the BN-PAS tie-up could mobilize Chinese voters toward PH and hurt BN in mixed seats.

**Onn Hafiz:** Defends Machap (won by 6,543 in 2022), backed by UMNO heavyweights, framing BN as "underdog" while targeting retention of 40 seats. No direct approval poll for him was found; the closest data point is a Merdeka Center survey ranking federal rival PM Anwar at 52% approval.

**Caveats:** Higher turnout is expected (Singapore border reopened, no Najib "Bossku" factor), which could aid PH if Chinese turnout rises. The election is seen as a national barometer ahead of GE16 (due by Feb 2028).

All findings, data points, and sources have been recorded in the structured output. Note: I was unable to fetch some primary pages (Wikipedia and several outlets returned 403 errors on direct fetch), so figures were cross-validated across AskNews article summaries and web-search snippets instead.

sources.title: Johor 2026 differs greatly from 2022 (Dai Zihao analysis)
sources.snippet: No Najib 'Bossku' factor; higher turnout expected; Malay turnout aids BN/PN, Chinese turnout aids PH.

key_facts: Polling day is July 11, 2026 (early voting July 7); assembly dissolved June 1, 2026 by caretaker MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi — a snap election ~1 year early.
All 56 DUN seats contested; 29 seats needed for a majority; ~2.72-2.73 million registered voters (54% Malay, 36% Chinese, 7% Indian, 3% others).
2022 baseline: BN 40, PH 12, PN 3, MUDA 1 on a low 54.92% turnout (vs 84.51% in 2018).
Candidate split: BN all 56 (37 UMNO/15 MCA/4 MIC), PH all 56 (20 PKR/19 Amanah/17 DAP), PN only 33 (then withdrew ~23, creating 14 straight BN-vs-PH fights); 172 total candidates vs 239 in 2022.
PAS (PN) directed supporters to vote BN in seats PN isn't contesting — consolidating the Malay-Muslim vote behind BN; widely called 'a gift to UMNO.'
Analyst consensus: BN strongly favored to retain the Johor government; FULCRUM titled its analysis 'Advantage Barisan Nasional.'
Teng Fong Securities scenarios: ~80% combined probability BN retains control (38-42 seats 40%, >40 seats 25%, PAS gains in a BN win 15%); only ~5% for an outright PN/opposition win.
Risk that the BN-PAS tie-up backfires by mobilizing Chinese voters toward PH; reopened Singapore border may raise turnout, which could aid PH if Chinese turnout rises.
Onn Hafiz defends Machap (won 2022 by 6,543 votes), frames BN as 'underdog,' targets retaining 40 seats; backed by UMNO heavyweights Zahid, Hishammuddin, Khairy at nomination.
Merdeka Center survey (Mar 12-Apr 9, 2026, n=1,209) ranked federal rival PM Anwar most popular leader at 52% approval; no direct Onn Hafiz approval poll located.

BN incumbency tools: RM20.75M Phase 4 Johor Kasih housing aid mid-campaign, 63-pledge manifesto with RM300M fund, 'Bangsa Johor' branding, MIC targeting >60% Indian support (Indian voters grew ~80,000 to ~200,000 since 2022).
Bersatu (PN) has collapsed: won only 2 of 33 seats in 2022 vs 8 in 2018, making an independent PN/green-wave breakthrough unlikely.
Election viewed as a national barometer ahead of GE16 (due by Feb 2028); a strong PH result could prompt Anwar to call a snap general election. Negeri Sembilan votes Aug 1, 2026.
